data_in.description: hashed spectrum feature vectors from upstream feature hashing stage

domain_knowledge: Nearest neighbor indexes trade construction time for query speed by organizing vectors in space to enable rapid similarity lookups without exhaustive comparisons.
Feature-hashed vectors reduce spectrum dimensionality while preserving spectral similarity relationships needed for clustering.
Index serialization format must support fast deserialization and efficient neighbor lookup during downstream sparse distance matrix construction.
The index structure is an intermediate data structure in a four-stage pipeline: hashing → indexing → sparse distance matrix → density-based clustering.

direct_checks: verify that the falcon repository (github.com/bittremieux/falcon or bittremieux-lab/falcon) contains source code implementing nearest neighbor index construction
verify that the implementation accepts hashed feature vectors as input (array, vector, or matrix format)
verify that the implementation produces a serialized index structure as output (file, binary object, or pickle format)
script_runs: execute the index construction module with valid hashed feature vectors and confirm no runtime errors occur
file_exists: confirm that the serialized index output can be loaded and contains index metadata (e.g., vector dimension, number of indexed points, index type)
verify that index construction completes without exhaustive pairwise comparisons (robust to parameter choices; implementation may use LSH, KD-tree, or other approximate NN strategies)
expert_review: confirm that the nearest neighbor index construction correctly implements a valid approximate or exact nearest neighbor search strategy appropriate for spectrum similarity searching
confirm that the index structure supports efficient similarity queries and is suitable for sparse pairwise distance matrix computation as described in the workflow
confirm that serialization and deserialization of the index preserves correctness and does not introduce numerical artifacts

cmd: import pickle; import numpy as np; vectors = np.load('{inputs.hashed_vectors}'); from sklearn.neighbors import NearestNeighbors; nn = NearestNeighbors(n_neighbors=5, algorithm='kd_tree'); nn.fit(vectors); pickle.dump(nn, open('nn_index.pkl', 'wb'))

methodology_summary: Load hashed spectrum feature vectors from upstream feature hashing stage
Construct nearest neighbor index structure using input vectors
Serialize and write index to persistent storage for downstream use
Validation: index file exists and is deserializable; index supports efficient neighbor query operations required for sparse distance matrix construction

task_description: Construct serialized nearest neighbor index structures from hashed spectrum feature vectors to enable fast similarity searching in spectrum clustering. Output the index as a binary or structured file suitable for downstream distance matrix computation.
task_objective: Implement nearest neighbor index construction from hashed feature vectors and serialize the resulting index structure.
title: Reconstruct the Nearest Neighbor Index Construction Layer of the falcon pipeline

workflow_description: 1. Load hashed spectrum feature vectors (low-dimensional representations produced by feature hashing of binned spectra). 2. Build nearest neighbor indexes using the vector input, selecting an appropriate nearest neighbor algorithm and index structure. 3. Serialize and persist the index to a file format suitable for fast retrieval and similarity search operations.
